questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a oscilloscope trace to check the key physics idea. A wave has a period of 0.3 s. What is its frequency?
options: 0.3 Hz
3 Hz
questions.explanation: Frequency is the reciprocal of period.
questions.senNote: Frequency is the reciprocal of period.

questions.explanation: Frequency is the reciprocal of period.
questions.senNote: Frequency is the reciprocal of period.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the image formed by a convex lens
questions.question: A learner uses a convex lens ray diagram to check the key physics idea. A convex lens forms an image of a distant object. Which description is correct?
options: The image is virtual and upright.
The image is always larger than the object.
The image cannot be formed by refraction.
The image is real and can be focused onto a screen.
questions.correctAnswer: The image is real and can be focused onto a screen.
questions.explanation: A convex lens can form a real image that can be projected onto a screen when the object is beyond the focal length.
questions.senNote: A convex lens can form a real image that can be projected onto a screen when the object is beyond the focal length.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a plane mirror ray diagram to check the key physics idea. A ray of light hits a mirror at an angle of incidence of 25°. What is the angle of reflection?
questions.explanation: The angle of reflection equals the angle of incidence.
questions.senNote: The angle of reflection equals the angle of incidence.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a speaker amplitude demonstration to check the key physics idea. Which change makes a sound louder?
options: Increasing the wavelength only
Reducing the frequency
Making the speed zero
Increasing the amplitude of the wave
questions.correctAnswer: Increasing the amplitude of the wave
questions.explanation: Loudness depends on amplitude. A larger amplitude means more energy is transferred.
questions.senNote: Loudness depends on amplitude. A larger amplitude means more energy is transferred.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a slinky wave demonstration to check the key physics idea. Which statement about transverse and longitudinal waves is correct?
options: All waves are transverse in air.
Sound waves are transverse and light waves are longitudinal.
Light waves need particles to carry them.
Light waves are transverse, but sound waves in air are longitudinal.
questions.correctAnswer: Light waves are transverse, but sound waves in air are longitudinal.
questions.explanation: Light is a transverse electromagnetic wave. Sound in air is longitudinal.
questions.senNote: Light is a transverse electromagnetic wave. Sound in air is longitudinal.
